description: Products or brand elements arranged in a deliberate repeating grid or symmetrical flatlay — identical cups, stacked boxes, arrayed products — creating a visually satisfying pattern out of the product itself.
structural_recipe: Strict overhead (top-down) shot. Products arranged in rows, grids, or radial symmetry. Colour usually monochromatic or brand-palette locked. No human presence. Minimal props outside the repeated product itself. Often used for new collection launches or seasonal variety shots.
why_it_works: Stops the scroll through visual order and abundance signals. The repetition suggests scale, variety, and generosity — all resonant Saudi hosting values. Works especially well for seasonal specials (Ramadan boxes, Eid bundles) where showing breadth matters.
transplantation_caveats: Requires products with strong individual visual appeal — flat or poorly designed packaging kills the effect.
Background must contrast cleanly with product; warm neutrals or brand colours work best.
Risk of looking clinical/catalogue if lighting is too flat — add depth with directional light.
Less effective for single-SKU accounts with no product range to repeat.

provenance.source: corpus_synthesis_474_obs — 11 observed uses across all sectors
